nodes.context: Mexican drug cartel based in Tijuana, Baja California, founded by the Arellano-Félix family.
Mexican drug cartel, rival and later ally of Tijuana Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel that aligned with Tijuana Cartel in 2016.
Mexican drug cartel that joined anti-Sinaloa alliance in 2016.
Predecessor cartel founded by Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo, broke up into Tijuana, Juarez, and Sinaloa cartels.
Mexican drug cartel, rival of Tijuana Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel, rival of Tijuana Cartel.
Mexican criminal group, rival of Sinaloa Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel that joined forces with Tijuana Cartel in 2003.
Colombian drug cartel, ally of Tijuana Cartel in the 1990s.
Colombian drug cartel, ally of Tijuana Cartel in the 1990s.
U.S. street gang, ally of Tijuana Cartel.
Organized crime group, ally of Tijuana Cartel.
Colombian guerrilla group with ties to Tijuana Cartel.
Armed wing of Tijuana Cartel operating in the United States.
Mexican drug cartel based in Tijuana, Baja California, involved in trafficking cocaine, marijuana, methamphetamine, and heroin into the United States.
Mexican drug cartel that gained control of the Tijuana/San Diego route after the Tijuana Cartel's decline.
Mexican drug cartel that heads an anti-Sinaloa alliance with Tijuana Cartel and Beltrán-Leyva Organization.
Mexican drug cartel that aligned with Tijuana Cartel and Jalisco New Generation Cartel in an anti-Sinaloa alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: since the 2006 Sinaloa Cartel incursion into Baja California and the fall of the Arellano-Félix brothers, the Tijuana Cartel has been reduced to a few cells.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel incursion into Tijuana territory in 2006, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, the organization became known as Cartel Tijuana Nueva Generación (New Generation Tijuana Cartel) and began to align itself under the Jalisco New Generation Cartel
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel aligned under Jalisco New Generation Cartel in 2016.

edges.evidence_quote: along with the Beltrán-Leyva Organization (BLO) to create an anti-Sinaloa alliance
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel and BLO formed an anti-Sinaloa alliance headed by Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: This alliance has since dwindled as the Tijuana, Jalisco New Generation, and Sinaloa cartels all now battle each other for trafficking influence
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel battles Sinaloa Cartel for trafficking influence.

edges.evidence_quote: This alliance has since dwindled as the Tijuana, Jalisco New Generation, and Sinaloa cartels all now battle each other for trafficking influence
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel battles Jalisco New Generation Cartel for trafficking influence.

edges.evidence_quote: Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo, the founder of the Guadalajara Cartel, was arrested in 1989. ... his old organization broke up into three factions: the Tijuana Cartel
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel was a successor faction of the Guadalajara Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The relative peace in the city of Tijuana in 2010–2012 has raised speculations of a possible agreement between the Tijuana Cartel and the Sinaloa Cartel to maintain peace
edges.context: Possible truce or agreement between Tijuana and Sinaloa cartels for peace.

edges.evidence_quote: the Tijuana cartel charges a toll ("piso") on the Sinaloa cartel for trafficking drugs in their territory
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel charges Sinaloa Cartel a toll for trafficking in its territory, indicating a cooperative arrangement.

edges.evidence_quote: an alliance with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, which also resulted in the cartel being rebranded as Tijuana Cartel New Generation ... but this alliance disintegrated very quickly when the Jalisco Cartel showed it intended to take the place of the Sinaloa cartel
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel formed an alliance with Jalisco New Generation Cartel, but it disintegrated due to Jalisco's intentions.

edges.evidence_quote: the Tijuana Cartel reversed their branding back to Cartel Arellano Félix and arranged the assassination of Cabo 33 the Jalisco Cartel's head of operations in Baja California
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel assassinated a Jalisco Cartel leader, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: the Tijuana Cartel has further engaged the Sinaloa Cartel for control of Tijuana plaza
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel engages Sinaloa Cartel for control of Tijuana plaza.

edges.evidence_quote: emissaries from the Los Chapitos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel had been seen in Tijuana attempting to form an alliance with the now resurgent Tijuana Cartel
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel faction attempted to form an alliance with Tijuana Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The Oaxaca Cartel reportedly joined forces with the Tijuana Cartel in 2003.
edges.context: Oaxaca Cartel joined forces with Tijuana Cartel in 2003.

edges.evidence_quote: Fourteen Mexican drug gang members were killed ... in a gun battle in Tijuana ... that was one of the bloodiest shootouts in the narco-war between the Tijuana Cartel and the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Gun battle between Tijuana and Sinaloa cartels in 2008.

edges.evidence_quote: captured cartel lieutenant Francisco Sillas Rocha had confessed that the Tijuana Cartel and the Sinaloa Cartel had formed a truce and that the Tijuana Cartel was seeking to merge with the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel formed a truce and sought merger with Sinaloa Cartel in 2011.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 1990s they formed an alliance with the Cali Cartel and Norte del Valle Cartel for the cocaine business.
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel allied with Cali Cartel for cocaine business in the 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 1990s they formed an alliance with the Cali Cartel and Norte del Valle Cartel for the cocaine business.
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel allied with Norte del Valle Cartel for cocaine business in the 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Tijuana Cartel is believed to have ties with FARC, a Colombia guerrilla group
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel has ties with FARC.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Palillos ("The Toothpicks") was a group operating within Tijuana Cartel, who worked as the armed wing of the Tijuana Cartel
edges.context: Los Palillos was the armed wing of Tijuana Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Logan Heights Gang, Russian mafia, Cali Cartel (defunct), Norte del Valle Cartel (defunct), Oaxaca Cartel (defunct)
edges.context: Logan Heights Gang is an ally of Tijuana Cartel per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Logan Heights Gang, Russian mafia, Cali Cartel (defunct), Norte del Valle Cartel (defunct), Oaxaca Cartel (defunct)
edges.context: Russian mafia is an ally of Tijuana Cartel per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, Gulf Cartel, Juárez Cartel
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel is a rival of Tijuana Cartel per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, Gulf Cartel, Juárez Cartel
edges.context: Gulf Cartel is a rival of Tijuana Cartel per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, Gulf Cartel, Juárez Cartel
edges.context: Juarez Cartel is a rival of Tijuana Cartel per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: the Sinaloa Cartel outweighed the falling Arellano-Felix Organization and gained control of the Tijuana/ San Diego route.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel took over territory and routes from the weakened Tijuana Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the Tijuana Cartel has been reduced to a few cells. In 2016, the organization became known as Cartel Tijuana Nueva Generación (New Generation Tijuana Cartel) and began to align itself under the Jalisco New Generation Cartel
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel aligned under Jalisco New Generation Cartel as part of an anti-Sinaloa alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: the Tijuana Cartel has been reduced to a few cells. In 2016, the organization became known as Cartel Tijuana Nueva Generación (New Generation Tijuana Cartel) and began to align itself under the Jalisco New Generation Cartel , along with the Beltrán-Leyva Organization (BLO) to create an anti-Sinaloa alliance
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel and Beltrán-Leyva Organization allied under Jalisco New Generation Cartel against Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the Tijuana Cartel has been reduced to a few cells. In 2016, the organization became known as Cartel Tijuana Nueva Generación (New Generation Tijuana Cartel) and began to align itself under the Jalisco New Generation Cartel , along with the Beltrán-Leyva Organization (BLO) to create an anti-Sinaloa alliance, which the Jalisco New Generation Cartel heads.
edges.context: Jalisco New Generation Cartel headed an anti-Sinaloa alliance that included Beltrán-Leyva Organization.

edges.evidence_quote: This alliance has since dwindled as the Tijuana, Jalisco New Generation, and Sinaloa cartels all now battle each other for trafficking influence in the city of Tijuana and the region of Baja California.
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel battles Sinaloa Cartel for trafficking influence.

edges.evidence_quote: This alliance has since dwindled as the Tijuana, Jalisco New Generation, and Sinaloa cartels all now battle each other for trafficking influence in the city of Tijuana and the region of Baja California.
edges.context: Jalisco New Generation Cartel battles Sinaloa Cartel for trafficking influence.
